title: Aptitude: Trigonometry & Height and Distances - Q5

content.problemStatement: ### **Aptitude Practice Question** The The angle of elevation of the top of a tower from point A on the ground is 45°. On moving 20 meters toward the tower, the angle of elevation of the top of the tower becomes 60°. Find the height of the tower (in meters). Let h be the height of the tower and x be the distance between the initial position and the foot of the tower. From the right-angled triangle AOB, where O is the foot of the tower, we have: tan(45°) = h / x ⇒ h = x From the right-angled triangle COB, where C is the new position of the observer, we have: tan(60°) = h / (x - 20) ⇒ h = (x - 20) × √3 Equating both expressions for h, we get: x = √3(x - 20) x(1-√3) = -20√3 x=20√3/(√3-1) = 47.32m Therefore, the height of the tower is approximately 47.32 meters (rounded to two decimal places).
